examples.title: CFG Construction for Union of CFLs
examples.description: Algorithm to construct CFG for union of two CFLs
examples.explanation: Creates a new grammar with a new start symbol that can derive either the first language or the second language, proving closure under union.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n1 + n2) where n1, n2 are production counts

examples.title: CFG Construction for Concatenation of CFLs
examples.description: Algorithm to construct CFG for concatenation of two CFLs
examples.explanation: Creates a new grammar where the start symbol derives the concatenation of strings from both languages by having S1 S2 as the only production from the new start symbol.

examples.title: CFG Construction for Kleene Star of CFL
examples.description: Algorithm to construct CFG for L* where L is CFL
examples.explanation: The new start symbol can generate zero or more occurrences of the original language by recursively expanding S_new → S S_new, with epsilon as the base case for zero repetitions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is production count
